7.4 PARTIAL FRACTIONS

LAPLACE TRANSFORMS

f (t) F (s) 1 1/s t 1/s2 tm m!/sm+1 tν , (ν > −1) Γ(ν + 1)/sν+1 e−at 1/(s + a) sin bt b/(s2 + b2 ) cos bt s/(s2 + b2 ) sinh bt b/(s2 − b2 ) cosh bt s/(s2 − b2 ) sin bt − bt cos bt 2b3 /(s2 + b2 )2 sin bt + bt cos bt 2bs2 /(s2 + b2 )2 t sin bt 2bs/(s2 + b2 )2 te−at 1/(s + a)2 u(t − c) e−cs /s e−at f (t) F (s + a) tf (t) −F 0 (s) f (t − c)u(t − c) e−cs F (s) f 0 (t) sF (s) − f (0) f 00 (t) s2 F (s) − sf (0) − f 0 (0) f 000 (t) s3 F (s) − s2 f (0) − sf 0 (0) − f 00 (0) Z t f (τ )dτ F (s)/s

We will now look at how the theory of partial fractions can be used to find the inverse Laplace transform of rational polynomials. This is a skill that you already have from integration theory and the methods transfer across without any change at all.

Example 1 Find the inverse Laplace transform of each of the following functions:

6s i) F (s) = s2 − 11s + 28

7s2 + s + 27 ii) F (s) = (s2 + 4)(s − 1)

5s2 − 36s + 23 iii) F (s) = (s − 7)2 (s + 1)

All of these are partial fraction questions. Recall that the two crucial features we need for parfrac to work on a rational function is factors on the bottom and for the rational function to be bottom heavy. If the degree of the top is greater than or equal to the degree on the bottom we simply do a little long division first.

6s 6s A B A(s − 7) + B(s − 4) i) = = + = . Thus s2 − 11s + 28 (s − 4)(s − 7) s−4 s−7 (s − 4)(s − 7) A(s − 7) + B(s − 4) ≡ 6s

7s2 + s + 27 As + B C (As + B)(s − 1) + C(s2 + 4) ii) = + = . Thus (s2 + 4)(s − 1) s2 + 4 s−1 (s2 + 4)(s − 1)

(As + B)(s − 1) + C(s2 + 4) ≡ 7s2 + s + 27

iii) For repeated factors we need to be very careful with both the decomposition and the recomposition.

5s2 − 36s + 23 A B C A(s − 7)(s + 1) + B(s + 1) + C(s − 7)2 = + + = . (s − 7)2 (s + 1) (s − 7) (s − 7)2 (s + 1) (s − 7)2 (s + 1) Thus A(s − 7)(s + 1) + B(s + 1) + C(s − 7)2 ≡ 5s2 − 36s + 23

F i) 14e7t − 8e4t ii) sin(2t) + 7et iii) (2t + 4)e7t + e−t F

We conclude with a lovely little proof that uses one of our earlier methods: Leibniz’ rule.
